fields.helpText: DEA uses an alpha character business activity code to identify various  categories of registrants. For example business activity code "C"  indicates a "Practitioner". DEA added a business activity sub-code field  to identify new sub-categories of registrants. For example business activity code "C" in combination with business activity sub-code "1" indicates a practitioner who has received a Drug Addiction Treatment Act (DATA) Waiver (DATA Waived or DW) to provide office based opioid treatment to

This field holds the Business Activity value used for DEA numbers. This  value can currently be one of the following: A,B,C,D,E,F,G,H,J,K,L,M,N,P,R,S,T or U   This value, combined with a numeric business sub-type, categorizes a DEA  number.

DEA uses an alpha character business activity code to identify various  categories of registrants. For example business activity code "C"  indicates a "Practitioner". DEA added a business activity sub-code field to identify new sub-categories of registrants. For example business activity code "C" in combination with business activity sub-code "1" indicates a practitioner who has received a Drug Addiction Treatment Act (DATA) Waiver (DATA Waived or DW) to provide office based opioid treatment to
This is the description given for the Business Activity Code and Sub-code.
This field should only be populated when a new Business Activity Code (BAC) is entered manually due to the failure of the PSO DOJ/DEA WEB  SERVICE to establish a connection to the DOJ web server.
